File: /Users/sanjaybasu/clinical-formal-verification/experiments/runs_compute/_judge_prompts/compute-m032-001.txt

The deeply-nested if-then-else on each step event is just repeated subtraction of 32, which computes a modular linear recurrence over acc in [0,31]: step_0 = (3*acc+20) mod 32, step_1 = (3*acc+6) mod 32, step_2 = (3*acc+18) mod 32. (Verified the nested form equals mod-32 for all acc in [0,31]; at most 3 reductions are needed since 3*31+20 = 113 < 128.)

The faulty resume path t_resume_glitch fires on send_attempt when (opted_out and acc == 18), setting sent := true, which violates the global invariant opted_out implies not sent. The target acc value is therefore 18.

acc starts at 0. optout does not modify acc, and step events after optout still update acc. So I need a step sequence from acc=0 reaching acc=18. A single step_2 from acc=0 gives (3*0+18) mod 32 = 18 — the target is reachable.

Full simulation of the witness sequence:
- step_2: opted_out=false, sent=false, acc=0 -> 18
- optout: opted_out=true, sent=false, acc=18 (guard t_optout always true)
- send_attempt: opted_out=true and acc==18 -> t_resume_glitch fires -> sent=true

Final state opted_out=true AND sent=true violates globally(opted_out implies not sent). The sequence is 3 events, well within the 34-event bound. Property is VIOLATED.
